title: Advanced FFI Types
text: Array returns use structured return specification rather bare type string. implemented form : type: one supported array types length_arg: argument provides returned length free: whether wrapper free() returned C buffer copying wrapper copies returned buffer fresh R vector. hand C buffer R reference.
code: ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(     "     #include <stdlib.h>      int* dup_array(int* x, int n) {       if (n <= 0) return NULL;       int* out = (int*)malloc(sizeof(int) * n);       for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) out[i] = x[i] * 2;       return out;     }     "   ) |>   tcc_bind(     dup_array = list(       args = list("integer_array", "i32"),       returns = list(type = "integer_array", length_arg = 2, free = TRUE)     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi$dup_array(as.integer(c(1, 2, 3)), 3L) #> [1] 2 4 6

title: Advanced FFI Types
text: Callback arguments use callback:<signature> callback_async:<signature>. callback object owns registered R function. callback pointer user-data token passed generated trampoline. callback_async:<signature> specifically: registry currently supports 256 live callbacks i64, u32, u64 async arguments returns travel R numeric (double), exact integer values 2^53 round-trip exactly async callbacks run main thread services R’s event loop, call tcc_callback_async_drain() explicitly tight loops tests
code: cb <- tcc_callback(function(x) x * 3, "double (*)(double)") cb_ptr <- tcc_callback_ptr(cb)  ffi_cb <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(     "     double apply_cb(double (*cb)(void* ctx, double), void* ctx, double x) {       return cb(ctx, x);     }     "   ) |>   tcc_bind(     apply_cb = list(       args = list("callback:double(double)", "ptr", "f64"),       returns = "f64"     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi_cb$apply_cb(cb, cb_ptr, 5) #> [1] 15 tcc_callback_close(cb)

title: Advanced FFI Types
text: tcc_global() generates getter setter wrappers C globals. Globals limited scalar FFI types. Array globals rejected API.
code: ffi_global <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(     "     int global_counter = 7;     "   ) |>   tcc_global("global_counter", "i32") |>   tcc_compile()  ffi_global$global_global_counter_get() #> [1] 7 ffi_global$global_global_counter_set(9L) #> [1] 9 ffi_global$global_global_counter_get() #> [1] 9

title: Advanced FFI Types
text: C structs, Rtinycc can generate allocation, getter, setter, free helpers. helpers separate tcc_bind() expose storage layout rather just call signatures.
code: ffi_struct <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(     "     struct point {       double x;       double y;     };     "   ) |>   tcc_struct("point", accessors = c(x = "f64", y = "f64")) |>   tcc_compile()  pt <- ffi_struct$struct_point_new() pt <- ffi_struct$struct_point_set_x(pt, 1.5) pt <- ffi_struct$struct_point_set_y(pt, 2.5) ffi_struct$struct_point_get_x(pt) #> [1] 1.5 ffi_struct$struct_point_free(pt) #> NULL

text: measures module build time, call time. expected pattern : Rtinycc wins clearly tiny compile latency stays -process shell R CMD SHLIB callme pays ordinary shared-library toolchain cost
code: compile_times <- data.frame(   implementation = c("Rtinycc", "callme"),   seconds = c(     median_elapsed(build_rtinycc_module(), times = 3L),     median_elapsed(build_and_dispose_callme_module(), times = 3L)   ) )  compile_times$milliseconds <- round(compile_times$seconds * 1000, 1) compile_times #>   implementation seconds milliseconds #> 1        Rtinycc   0.016           16 #> 2         callme   0.202          202

title: Compilation and Call Overhead
text: generated code makes extra return-path work explicit. particular, rand_unif() wrapper allocates R vector, memcpy()s native double* buffer , free()s original buffer. contrast, fill_rand() uses borrowed numeric_array input path.
code: Rtinycc:::rtinycc_c_block(generated_code) /* TinyCC workaround: _Complex not supported */ #define _Complex  #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h> #ifndef STRING_PTR_RO #define STRING_PTR_RO STRING_PTR #endif void RC_free_finalizer(SEXP ext); void RC_owned_native_finalizer(SEXP ext); SEXP RC_make_borrowed_view(void *ptr, SEXP tag, SEXP owner); SEXP RC_make_unowned_ptr(void *ptr, SEXP tag); SEXP RC_make_owned_ptr(void *ptr, SEXP tag); SEXP RC_make_owned_composite_ptr(void *ptr, SEXP tag);  #include <stdint.h> #include <stdbool.h> #include <stddef.h> #include <limits.h> #include <math.h> #include <string.h>  /* User code */ #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h> #include <Rmath.h> #include <stdlib.h>  void noop(void) {}  void fill_rand(double* out, int n) {   if (n < 0) {     Rf_error("n must be non-negative");   }    GetRNGstate();   for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {     out[i] = unif_rand();   }   PutRNGstate(); }  double* rand_unif(int n) {   if (n < 0) {     Rf_error("n must be non-negative");   }   if (n == 0) {     return (double*) malloc(sizeof(double));   }    double *out = (double*) malloc(sizeof(double) * (size_t) n);   if (!out) {     Rf_error("malloc failed");   }    GetRNGstate();   for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {     out[i] = unif_rand();   }   PutRNGstate();   return out; }  /* R callable wrappers for bound symbols */ SEXP R_wrap_noop(void) {   // No arguments    // Call and return    noop();      return R_NilValue; }   SEXP R_wrap_fill_rand(SEXP arg1_, SEXP arg2_) {   if (TYPEOF(arg1_) != REALSXP) Rf_error("expected numeric vector for argument 'arg1'");   double* arg1 = REAL(arg1_);   int _arg2 = asInteger(arg2_);   if (_arg2 == NA_INTEGER) Rf_error("integer value is NA");   if (_arg2 < INT32_MIN || _arg2 > INT32_MAX) Rf_error("i32 out of range");   int32_t arg2 = (int32_t)_arg2;    // Call and return    fill_rand(arg1, arg2);      return R_NilValue; }   SEXP R_wrap_rand_unif(SEXP arg1_) {   int _arg1 = asInteger(arg1_);   if (_arg1 == NA_INTEGER) Rf_error("integer value is NA");   if (_arg1 < INT32_MIN || _arg1 > INT32_MAX) Rf_error("i32 out of range");   int32_t arg1 = (int32_t)_arg1;    // Call and return    double* __rtinycc_ret = rand_unif(arg1);    if (!__rtinycc_ret) return R_NilValue;    SEXP out = PROTECT(allocVector(REALSXP, arg1));      if (arg1 > 0) memcpy(REAL(out), __rtinycc_ret, sizeof(double) * arg1);      if (__rtinycc_ret) free(__rtinycc_ret);      UNPROTECT(1);      return out; }

title: Header Parsing with treesitter.c

title: Header Parsing with treesitter.c
text: fuller workflow, use tcc_generate_bindings() tcc_ffi object already matching C source attached: keeps parsing step separate actual compilation step, important want inspect edit generated binding plan first.

Provides dimension-independent array primitives (dim, shape, sel, reshape, take, drop, rotate, cat), element-wise arithmetic (ArrayArith), reductions (ArrayReduce: sum, prod, minval, maxval via fold), transforms (ArrayTransform: transpose, , mask). built -loops (SAC’s genarray/modarray comprehensions) ψ-reduction (condensation) — technique fusing consecutive array operations single loops never materialize intermediate arrays. pipeline: R AST → declare parse → walk + lower → IR → codegen → TinyCC.

title: Rtinycc Agent Guidelines (Short)
text: core codegen mechanism tccq_cg_vec_elem(node, idx_var), recursively defines k-th element vector expression tree terms children’s k-th elements. ψ-reduction (Mullin 1988) / condensation (Schmittgen 1986) described Scholz’s SAC paper: consecutive array operations fused single loop body computes element without materializing intermediates. example, sum(x[:(+n-1)] * weights) compiles single loop: slice array allocated, product array allocated. composition reduce(binary(vec_slice, var)) resolved entirely recursive element access. Correspondence SAC:
code: for (k = 0; k < len; ++k) acc += p_x[(i-1)+k] * p_weights[k];

title: Rtinycc Agent Guidelines (Short)
text: tcc_quick() API exported working. Declaration parsing (R/tcc_quick_declare.R), codetools-style walker (R/tcc_quick_walk.R), lowering (R/tcc_quick_lower.R), codegen (R/tcc_quick_codegen.R), caching (R/tcc_quick_cache.R) implemented. IR node constructors R/tcc_ir.R; R→C function registry R/tcc_ir_registry.R (39 entries covering math.h Rmath.h). Registry-driven dispatch: lowerer checks tcc_ir_c_api_registry() function call emits call1 (arity-1) call2 (arity-2) IR nodes. Adding new math function requires one registry entry, dispatcher codegen changes. Scalar lowering/codegen: arithmetic, comparisons, logical ops, /ifelse, unary math, casts, stop. Vector operations: vec_alloc, vec_get/vec_set, vec_slice (range view x[:b]), vec_rewrite (element-wise -place reassignment), element-wise binary/unary vectors, rev, pmin/pmax, raw vectors (raw(n), .raw, type(... = raw(...))). Typed mapping subset: sapply(x, FUN) supported symbol FUN; apply(X, MARGIN, FUN) matrix X, literal MARGIN {1,2}, FUN {sum, mean} (native row/col reducers direct matrix-variable inputs, delegated fallback otherwise). Reductions: reduce (named variable), reduce_expr (arbitrary vector expression — condensation path), which_reduce, /(short-circuit). Native stats reducers: mean, sd, median, quantile (scalar vector probs), including literal na.rm = TRUE/FALSE handling. Cumulative operations: cumsum, cumprod, cummax, cummin (sequential scan, condensable). Matrix: mat_alloc, mat_get, mat_set, nrow, ncol. Native matrix row/col reducers: rowSums, colSums, rowMeans, colMeans (including literal na.rm). Multidimensional declaration space: rank-3+ types parsed/tracked array shape currently reserved (fallback soft/auto, hard error hard). BLAS-backed matrix products: %*%, crossprod, tcrossprod lower matmul codegen F77_CALL(dgemm) via R_ext/BLAS.h. LAPACK-backed linear solve: direct solve(, b) / solve(, B) lowers solve_lin codegen F77_CALL(dgesv). Windows BLAS linkage note: matmul present, tcc_quick_compile() links Rblas explicitly (addition R) dgemm often resolves Rblas.dll. Debug diagnostics: tcc_quick(..., debug = TRUE) prints compile diagnostics including OS, has_matmul, linked libraries, library paths. Control flow: (seq_along/seq_len/seq_range), , repeat, break, next, /-else statements. Boundary detection (.Call, .C, .External, .Internal, .Primitive) fallback policy (auto/soft/hard; aliases always/never). Delegated evaluation environment: rf_call evaluates wrapper call environment (environment()), fixed R_GlobalEnv. IR validation pass: hard mode rejects rf_call path; malformed IR nodes fail fast. ALTREP-safe codegen: read-input parameters use REAL_RO()/INTEGER_RO()/LOGICAL_RO() const pointer qualifiers. Parameters mutated (targeted vec_set/mat_set/vec_rewrite) Rf_duplicate()d coercion avoid corrupting caller’s objects. Mutation tracking handled tccq_scope_mark_mutated() lowerer propagated ir$mutated_params. Capability table helpers available R/tcc_ir_registry.R (tcc_rapi_table(), tcc_rapi_has(), tcc_rapi_summary(), tcc_quick_rf_call_allowlist(), tcc_quick_rf_call_quiet()). Tests: test_tcc_quick_scalar.R (11), test_tcc_quick_vector.R (23), test_tcc_quick_matrix.R (18), test_tcc_quick_control.R (6), test_tcc_quick_fallback.R (14), test_tcc_quick_ops.R (30). Full package suite currently runs 350 tests.

Rtinycc never replicates platform-specific layout rules. wrappers can also link external shared libraries whose symbols TCC resolves relocation time. background compares libffi approach, see RSimpleFFI README. macOS configure script strips -flat_namespace TCC’s build avoid BUS ERROR issues. Windows, configure.win script generates UCRT-backed msvcrt.def TinyCC resolves CRT symbols ucrtbase.dll (R 4.2+ uses UCRT). Ownership semantics explicit. Pointers tcc_malloc() tagged rtinycc_owned can released tcc_free() (R finalizer). Generated struct constructors use struct-specific tag (struct_<name>) RC_free_finalizer; free struct_<name>_free(), tcc_free(). Pointers tcc_data_ptr() tagged rtinycc_borrowed never freed Rtinycc. Array returns copied fresh R vector; set free = TRUE C function returns malloc-owned buffer.

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: can compile call C functions entirely memory. simplest path quick JIT compilation. lower-level API gives full control include paths, libraries, R C API. Using #define _Complex workaround TCC’s lack complex type support, can link R’s headers call libR.
code: state <- tcc_state(output = "memory") tcc_compile_string(state, "int forty_two(){ return 42; }") #> [1] 0 tcc_relocate(state) #> [1] 0 tcc_call_symbol(state, "forty_two", return = "int") #> [1] 42 state <- tcc_state(output = "memory") tcc_add_include_path(state, R.home("include")) #> [1] 0 tcc_add_library_path(state, R.home("lib")) #> [1] 0  code <- ' #define _Complex #include <R.h> #include <Rinternals.h>  double call_r_sqrt(void) {   SEXP fn   = PROTECT(Rf_findFun(Rf_install("sqrt"), R_BaseEnv));   SEXP val  = PROTECT(Rf_ScalarReal(16.0));   SEXP call = PROTECT(Rf_lang2(fn, val));   SEXP out  = PROTECT(Rf_eval(call, R_GlobalEnv));   double res = REAL(out)[0];   UNPROTECT(4);   return res; } ' tcc_compile_string(state, code) #> [1] 0 tcc_relocate(state) #> [1] 0 tcc_call_symbol(state, "call_r_sqrt", return = "double") #> [1] 4

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: Rtinycc ships set typed memory access functions similar ctypesio package offers, designed around FFI pointer model. Every scalar C type corresponding tcc_read_* / tcc_write_* pair operates byte offset external pointer, can walk structs, arrays, output parameters without writing C helpers. Typed reads writes cover full scalar range (i8/u8, i16/u16, i32/u32, i64/u64, f32/f64) plus pointer dereferencing via tcc_read_ptr / tcc_write_ptr. operations use byte offset memcpy internally alignment safety. Pointer--pointer workflows supported C APIs return values output parameters.
code: ptr <- tcc_cstring("hello") tcc_read_cstring(ptr) #> [1] "hello" tcc_read_bytes(ptr, 5) #> [1] 68 65 6c 6c 6f tcc_ptr_addr(ptr, hex = TRUE) #> [1] "0x5b307d43dc30" tcc_ptr_is_null(ptr) #> [1] FALSE tcc_free(ptr) #> NULL buf <- tcc_malloc(32) tcc_write_i32(buf, 0L, 42L) tcc_write_f64(buf, 8L, pi) tcc_read_i32(buf, offset = 0L) #> [1] 42 tcc_read_f64(buf, offset = 8L) #> [1] 3.141593 tcc_free(buf) #> NULL ptr_ref <- tcc_malloc(.Machine$sizeof.pointer %||% 8L) target <- tcc_malloc(8) tcc_ptr_set(ptr_ref, target) #> <pointer: 0x5b307d335050> tcc_data_ptr(ptr_ref) #> <pointer: 0x5b307cc3ef00> tcc_ptr_set(ptr_ref, tcc_null_ptr()) #> <pointer: 0x5b307d335050> tcc_free(target) #> NULL tcc_free(ptr_ref) #> NULL

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: FFI exposes small set type mappings R C. Conversions explicit predictable callers know data shared versus copied. scalar type names C-facing, R-side carriers one--one C widths: i8, i16, i32, u8, u16 mediated R integer scalars u32, i64, u64, f32, f64 mediated R numeric (double) coercion boxing bool uses R logical cstring uses R character scalar means u32 routed double preserve full unsigned 32-bit range, i64 / u64 exact 2^53 R side. Array arguments pass R vectors C zero copy: raw maps uint8_t*, integer_array int32_t*, numeric_array double*. Pointer types include ptr (opaque external pointer), sexp (pass SEXP directly), callback signatures like callback:double(double). Variadic functions supported two forms: typed prefix tails (varargs) bounded dynamic tails (varargs_types + varargs_min/varargs_max). Prefix mode cheaper runtime path dispatch tail arity ; bounded dynamic mode adds per-call scalar type inference select compatible wrapper. hot loops, prefer fixed arity first, prefix variadics tight maximum tail size. Array returns use returns = list(type = "integer_array", length_arg = 2, free = TRUE) copy result new R vector. length_arg 1-based index C argument carries array length. Set free = TRUE C function returns malloc-owned buffer.

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: Rtinycc supports two ways bind variadic tails. legacy approach uses varargs typed prefix tail, bounded dynamic approach uses varargs_types together varargs_min varargs_max. bounded mode, wrappers generated across allowed arity type combinations, runtime dispatch selects matching wrapper scalar tail values provided call time.
code: ffi_var <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_header("#include <R_ext/Print.h>") |>   tcc_source('     #include <stdarg.h>      int sum_fmt(int n, ...) {       va_list ap;       va_start(ap, n);       int s = 0;       for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += va_arg(ap, int);       va_end(ap);       Rprintf("sum_fmt(%d) = %d\\n", n, s);       return s;     }   ') |>   tcc_bind(     Rprintf = list(       args = list("cstring"),       variadic = TRUE,       varargs_types = list("i32"),       varargs_min = 0L,       varargs_max = 4L,       returns = "void"     ),     sum_fmt = list(       args = list("i32"),       variadic = TRUE,       varargs_types = list("i32"),       varargs_min = 0L,       varargs_max = 4L,       returns = "i32"     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi_var$Rprintf("Rprintf via bind: %d + %d = %d\n", 2L, 3L, 5L) #> Rprintf via bind: 2 + 3 = 5 #> NULL ffi_var$sum_fmt(0L) #> sum_fmt(0) = 0 #> [1] 0 ffi_var$sum_fmt(2L, 10L, 20L) #> sum_fmt(2) = 30 #> [1] 30 ffi_var$sum_fmt(4L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L) #> sum_fmt(4) = 10 #> [1] 10

text: R vectors passed C zero copy. Mutations C visible R.
code: ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source("     #include <stdlib.h>     #include <string.h>      int64_t sum_array(int32_t* arr, int32_t n) {       int64_t s = 0;       for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += arr[i];       return s;     }      void bump_first(int32_t* arr) { arr[0] += 10; }      int32_t* dup_array(int32_t* arr, int32_t n) {       int32_t* out = malloc(sizeof(int32_t) * n);       memcpy(out, arr, sizeof(int32_t) * n);       return out;     }   ") |>   tcc_bind(     sum_array  = list(args = list("integer_array", "i32"), returns = "i64"),     bump_first = list(args = list("integer_array"), returns = "void"),     dup_array  = list(       args = list("integer_array", "i32"),       returns = list(type = "integer_array", length_arg = 2, free = TRUE)     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  x <- as.integer(1:100) # to avoid ALTREP .Internal(inspect(x)) #> @5b307e5d8198 13 INTSXP g0c0 [REF(65535)]  1 : 100 (compact) ffi$sum_array(x, length(x)) #> [1] 5050  # Zero-copy: C mutation reflects in R ffi$bump_first(x) #> NULL x[1] #> [1] 11  # Array return: copied into a new R vector, C buffer freed y <- ffi$dup_array(x, length(x)) y[1] #> [1] 11  .Internal(inspect(x)) #> @5b307e5d8198 13 INTSXP g0c0 [REF(65535)]  11 : 110 (expanded)

code: ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source('     #include <math.h>     struct point { double x; double y; };     double distance(struct point* a, struct point* b) {       double dx = a->x - b->x, dy = a->y - b->y;       return sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);     }   ') |>   tcc_library("m") |>   tcc_struct("point", accessors = c(x = "f64", y = "f64")) |>   tcc_bind(distance = list(args = list("ptr", "ptr"), returns = "f64")) |>   tcc_compile()  p1 <- ffi$struct_point_new() ffi$struct_point_set_x(p1, 0.0) #> <pointer: 0x5b307dbbd010> ffi$struct_point_set_y(p1, 0.0) #> <pointer: 0x5b307dbbd010>  p2 <- ffi$struct_point_new() ffi$struct_point_set_x(p2, 3.0) #> <pointer: 0x5b307ae7f0c0> ffi$struct_point_set_y(p2, 4.0) #> <pointer: 0x5b307ae7f0c0>  ffi$distance(p1, p2) #> [1] 5  ffi$struct_point_free(p1) #> NULL ffi$struct_point_free(p2) #> NULL

text: R functions can registered C function pointers via tcc_callback() passed compiled code. Specify callback:<signature> argument tcc_bind() trampoline generated automatically. Call tcc_callback_close() want deterministic invalidation earlier release preserved R function.
code: cb <- tcc_callback(function(x) x * x, signature = "double (*)(double)")  code <- ' double apply_fn(double (*fn)(void* ctx, double), void* ctx, double x) {   return fn(ctx, x); } '  ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(code) |>   tcc_bind(     apply_fn = list(       args = list("callback:double(double)", "ptr", "f64"),       returns = "f64"     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi$apply_fn(cb, tcc_callback_ptr(cb), 7.0) #> [1] 49 tcc_callback_close(cb)

text: callback throws R error, trampoline catches , emits warning, returns type-appropriate sentinel instead unwinding C. practice means NA-like numeric integer values, NA logical, NULL cstring, null external pointer depending declared return type.
code: cb_err <- tcc_callback(   function(x) stop("boom"),   signature = "double (*)(double)" )  ffi_err <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source('     double call_cb_err(double (*cb)(void* ctx, double), void* ctx, double x) {       return cb(ctx, x);     }   ') |>   tcc_bind(     call_cb_err = list(       args = list("callback:double(double)", "ptr", "f64"),       returns = "f64"     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  warned <- FALSE res <- withCallingHandlers(   ffi_err$call_cb_err(cb_err, tcc_callback_ptr(cb_err), 1.0),   warning = function(w) {     warned <<- TRUE     invokeRestart("muffleWarning")   } ) list(warned = warned, result = res) #> $warned #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $result #> [1] NA tcc_callback_close(cb_err)

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: thread-safe scheduling worker threads, use callback_async:<signature> tcc_bind(). async callback queue initialized automatically package load. bound function callback_async: argument, generated wrapper automatically runs C function new thread draining callbacks main R thread. C code doesn’t need know draining — just call callback normal wrapper handles rest. Void return (fire--forget): callback enqueued thread executed main R thread automatically — Windows via R’s message pump, Linux/macOS via R’s event loop addInputHandler. Non-void return (synchronous): worker thread blocks main R thread executes callback returns real result. Supported return types: integer variants (int, int32_t, i8, i16, u8, u16), floating-point (double, float), bool/logical, pointer (void*, T*). Non-void return works way — generated wrapper handles drain loop transparently:
code: # Fire-and-forget: void callback accumulated from 100 worker threads hits <- 0L cb_async <- tcc_callback(   function(x) { hits <<- hits + x; NULL },   signature = "void (*)(int)" )  code_async <- ' struct task { void (*cb)(void* ctx, int); void* ctx; int value; };  #ifdef _WIN32 #include <windows.h>  static DWORD WINAPI worker(LPVOID data) {   struct task* t = (struct task*) data;   t->cb(t->ctx, t->value);   return 0; }  int spawn_async(void (*cb)(void* ctx, int), void* ctx, int value) {   if (!cb || !ctx) return -1;   struct task t;   t.cb = cb;   t.ctx = ctx;   t.value = value;   HANDLE th = CreateThread(NULL, 0, worker, &t, 0, NULL);   if (!th) return -2;   WaitForSingleObject(th, INFINITE);   CloseHandle(th);   return 0; } #else #include <pthread.h>  static void* worker(void* data) {   struct task* t = (struct task*) data;   t->cb(t->ctx, t->value);   return NULL; }  int spawn_async(void (*cb)(void* ctx, int), void* ctx, int value) {   if (!cb || !ctx) return -1;   const int n = 100;   struct task tasks[100];   pthread_t th[100];   for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {     tasks[i].cb = cb;     tasks[i].ctx = ctx;     tasks[i].value = value;     if (pthread_create(&th[i], NULL, worker, &tasks[i]) != 0) {       for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) pthread_join(th[j], NULL);       return -2;     }   }   for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) pthread_join(th[i], NULL);   return 0; } #endif '  ffi_async <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(code_async) if (.Platform$OS.type != "windows") {   ffi_async <- tcc_library(ffi_async, "pthread") } ffi_async <- ffi_async |>   tcc_bind(     spawn_async = list(       args = list("callback_async:void(int)", "ptr", "i32"),       returns = "i32"     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  rc <- ffi_async$spawn_async(cb_async, tcc_callback_ptr(cb_async), 2L) hits #> [1] 200 tcc_callback_close(cb_async) cb_triple <- tcc_callback(   function(x) x * 3L,   signature = "int (*)(int)" )  # Pure C: the worker calls the sync callback and returns its result. # No drain logic needed — the generated wrapper handles it. code_sync <- ' #ifdef _WIN32 #include <windows.h>  struct itask { int (*cb)(void*,int); void* ctx; int in; int out; };  static DWORD WINAPI iworker(LPVOID p) {   struct itask* t = (struct itask*)p;   t->out = t->cb(t->ctx, t->in);   return 0; } int run_worker(int (*cb)(void*,int), void* ctx, int x) {   struct itask t;   t.cb = cb; t.ctx = ctx; t.in = x; t.out = -1;   HANDLE th = CreateThread(NULL, 0, iworker, &t, 0, NULL);   if (!th) return -1;   WaitForSingleObject(th, INFINITE);   CloseHandle(th);   return t.out; } #else #include <pthread.h>  struct itask { int (*cb)(void*,int); void* ctx; int in; int out; };  static void* iworker(void* p) {   struct itask* t = (struct itask*)p;   t->out = t->cb(t->ctx, t->in);   return NULL; } int run_worker(int (*cb)(void*,int), void* ctx, int x) {   struct itask t;   t.cb = cb; t.ctx = ctx; t.in = x; t.out = -1;   pthread_t th;   if (pthread_create(&th, NULL, iworker, &t) != 0) return -1;   pthread_join(th, NULL);   return t.out; } #endif '  ffi_sync <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(code_sync) if (.Platform$OS.type != "windows") {   ffi_sync <- tcc_library(ffi_sync, "pthread") } ffi_sync <- ffi_sync |>   tcc_bind(     run_worker = list(args = list("callback_async:int(int)", "ptr", "i32"), returns = "i32")   ) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi_sync$run_worker(cb_triple, tcc_callback_ptr(cb_triple), 7L)  # 21 #> [1] 21 tcc_callback_close(cb_triple)

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: example ties together external library linking, callbacks, pointer dereferencing. open -memory SQLite database, execute queries, collect rows R callback reads char** arrays using tcc_read_ptr tcc_read_cstring.
code: ptr_size <- .Machine$sizeof.pointer  read_string_array <- function(ptr, n) {   vapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {     tcc_read_cstring(tcc_read_ptr(ptr, (i - 1L) * ptr_size))   }, "") }  cb <- tcc_callback(   function(argc, argv, cols) {     values <- read_string_array(argv, argc)     names  <- read_string_array(cols, argc)     cat(paste(names, values, sep = " = ", collapse = ", "), "\n")     0L   },   signature = "int (*)(int, char **, char **)" )  sqlite <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_header("#include <sqlite3.h>") |>   tcc_library("sqlite3") |>   tcc_source('     void* open_db() {       sqlite3* db = NULL;       sqlite3_open(":memory:", &db);       return db;     }     int close_db(void* db) {       return sqlite3_close((sqlite3*)db);     }   ') |>   tcc_bind(     open_db  = list(args = list(), returns = "ptr"),     close_db = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "i32"),     sqlite3_libversion = list(args = list(), returns = "cstring"),     sqlite3_exec = list(       args = list("ptr", "cstring", "callback:int(int, char **, char **)", "ptr", "ptr"),       returns = "i32"     )   ) |>   tcc_compile()  sqlite$sqlite3_libversion() #> [1] "3.45.1"  db <- sqlite$open_db() sqlite$sqlite3_exec(db, "CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER, name TEXT);", cb, tcc_callback_ptr(cb), tcc_null_ptr()) #> [1] 0 sqlite$sqlite3_exec(db, "INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, 'hello'), (2, 'world');", cb, tcc_callback_ptr(cb), tcc_null_ptr()) #> [1] 0 sqlite$sqlite3_exec(db, "SELECT * FROM t;", cb, tcc_callback_ptr(cb), tcc_null_ptr()) #> id = 1, name = hello  #> id = 2, name = world #> [1] 0 sqlite$close_db(db) #> [1] 0 tcc_callback_close(cb)

what: Stackless C Protothreads: streaming BCF/VCF records with htslib
title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: Rtinycc bundles Adam Dunkels’ Protothreads library, allowing JIT-compile cross-platform native iterators yield back R. demo binds htslib using protothread: R resumes C reader next bcf1_t ready, copies current fields regular R list. C logic never blocks R’s event loop, R objects created control returns safely main R stack. Important Protothreads Limitations: protothreads stackless (implemented using Duff’s device macro expansions), must observe strict rules C code: Local variables preserved across PT_YIELD calls. Store state context struct instead. nested blocking: yield inside nested C function call. switch statements: Protothreads uses switch internally, use switch within PT_THREAD block. demo uses plain VCF text, opened directly htslib API BCF. full demo source foldable . embedded htslib Protothreads C source extracted script rendered Rtinycc C-code display helper used vignettes. points toward useful separate package idea: small, generic Rtinycc-based C coroutine/Protothreads layer R. package provide reusable pieces like coro_new(), coro_resume(), finalizer-backed native handles, status/error conventions, helpers writing streaming bindings around C libraries htslib, SQLite, parsers, decompression libraries, network clients. R see ordinary iterators; C keep natural blocking recursive control flow without rewriting everything heap-allocated state machine.
code: source("scripts/demo-streaming-bcf-reader-ffi.R") run_streaming_bcf_demo() #> Rtinycc version: 0.1.11.9000 #> Demo: stackless Protothreads + htslib BCF/VCF API streaming reader #> Note: htslib reads run using Protothreads. Warning: Local C variables are NOT preserved across yields!; R objects are built only after each yield. #>  #> Input: generated VCF text (opened directly by htslib) #> Samples: sample1 #>  #> == Streaming records one resume at a time == #> record 1: chr1:10 id=rs1 ref=A alt=C qual=50 alleles=[A,C] #> record 2: chr1:20 id=. ref=G alt=A,T qual=99 alleles=[G,A,T] #> record 3: chr1:30 id=rs3 ref=TT alt=T qual=. alleles=[TT,T] #> done_after_collect=TRUE #!/usr/bin/env Rscript  suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(Rtinycc))   say <- function(...) cat(..., "\n", sep = "")  `%||%` <- function(x, y) if (is.null(x)) y else x  pkg_config_paths <- function(args, prefix) {   out <- tryCatch(     system2("pkg-config", c(args, "htslib"), stdout = TRUE, stderr = FALSE),     error = function(e) character()   )   out <- unlist(strsplit(paste(out, collapse = " "), "[[:space:]]+"))   out <- out[nzchar(out)]   sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", out[startsWith(out, prefix)]) }  build_streaming_bcf_ffi <- function() {   code <- ' #include <rtinycc/pt.h> #include <stdint.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> #include <stdio.h> #include <math.h> #include <htslib/hts.h> #include <htslib/vcf.h>  enum {   BCF_STREAM_ERR = -1,   BCF_STREAM_EOF = 0,   BCF_STREAM_RECORD = 1 };  typedef struct bcf_stream {   struct pt pt;   int yielded;   int started;   int done;   int error;   char err[256];   htsFile *fp;   bcf_hdr_t *hdr;   bcf1_t *rec;   char *alts;   size_t alts_cap;   int ret; } bcf_stream_t;  static void bcf_stream_set_error(bcf_stream_t *st, const char *msg) {   if (!st) return;   if (!msg) msg = "unknown htslib error";   snprintf(st->err, sizeof(st->err), "%s", msg);   st->error = 1; }  static int bcf_stream_resume_internal(bcf_stream_t *st) {   PT_BEGIN(&st->pt);    if (!st || !st->fp || !st->hdr || !st->rec) {     if (st) bcf_stream_set_error(st, "stream is not open");     if (st) st->done = 1;     PT_EXIT(&st->pt);   }    for (;;) {     st->ret = bcf_read(st->fp, st->hdr, st->rec);     if (st->ret == 0) {       if (bcf_unpack(st->rec, BCF_UN_STR) < 0) {         bcf_stream_set_error(st, "bcf_unpack() failed");         st->done = 1;         st->yielded = BCF_STREAM_ERR;         PT_EXIT(&st->pt);       }       st->yielded = BCF_STREAM_RECORD;       PT_YIELD(&st->pt);       if (st->error || st->done) PT_EXIT(&st->pt);     } else {       if (st->ret < -1) {         bcf_stream_set_error(st, "bcf_read() failed");       }       st->done = 1;       st->yielded = BCF_STREAM_EOF;       PT_EXIT(&st->pt);     }   }    PT_END(&st->pt); }  void *bcf_stream_open(const char *path) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) calloc(1, sizeof(bcf_stream_t));   if (!st) return NULL;    PT_INIT(&st->pt);    if (!path || !path[0]) {     bcf_stream_set_error(st, "path is empty");     st->done = 1;     return st;   }    st->fp = hts_open(path, "r");   if (!st->fp) {     bcf_stream_set_error(st, "hts_open() failed");     st->done = 1;     return st;   }    st->hdr = bcf_hdr_read(st->fp);   if (!st->hdr) {     bcf_stream_set_error(st, "bcf_hdr_read() failed");     st->done = 1;     return st;   }    st->rec = bcf_init();   if (!st->rec) {     bcf_stream_set_error(st, "bcf_init() failed");     st->done = 1;     return st;   }    return st; }  int bcf_stream_resume(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;    if (!st) return BCF_STREAM_ERR;   if (st->error) return BCF_STREAM_ERR;   if (st->done) return BCF_STREAM_EOF;    st->started = 1;   bcf_stream_resume_internal(st);    if (st->error) return BCF_STREAM_ERR;   if (st->done) return BCF_STREAM_EOF;   return st->yielded; }  int bcf_stream_done(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   return st ? st->done : 1; }  const char *bcf_stream_error(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st) return "stream pointer is NULL";   return st->err[0] ? st->err : NULL; }  const char *bcf_stream_chrom(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->hdr || !st->rec) return NULL;   return bcf_seqname(st->hdr, st->rec); }  int64_t bcf_stream_pos1(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->rec) return -1;   return (int64_t) st->rec->pos + 1; }  int64_t bcf_stream_end1(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->rec) return -1;   return (int64_t) st->rec->pos + (int64_t) st->rec->rlen; }  const char *bcf_stream_id(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->rec || !st->rec->d.id || !st->rec->d.id[0]) return ".";   return st->rec->d.id; }  int bcf_stream_n_allele(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->rec) return 0;   return st->rec->n_allele; }  const char *bcf_stream_allele(void *ptr, int idx) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->rec || idx < 0 || idx >= st->rec->n_allele) return NULL;   return st->rec->d.allele[idx]; }  const char *bcf_stream_ref(void *ptr) {   return bcf_stream_allele(ptr, 0); }  const char *bcf_stream_alt(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   size_t need = 2;   int i;    if (!st || !st->rec) return NULL;   if (st->rec->n_allele <= 1) return ".";    for (i = 1; i < st->rec->n_allele; ++i) {     const char *a = st->rec->d.allele[i] ? st->rec->d.allele[i] : "";     need += strlen(a) + 1;   }    if (need > st->alts_cap) {     char *tmp = (char *) realloc(st->alts, need);     if (!tmp) {       bcf_stream_set_error(st, "failed to allocate ALT buffer");       return NULL;     }     st->alts = tmp;     st->alts_cap = need;   }    st->alts[0] = 0;   for (i = 1; i < st->rec->n_allele; ++i) {     if (i > 1) strcat(st->alts, ",");     strcat(st->alts, st->rec->d.allele[i] ? st->rec->d.allele[i] : "");   }    return st->alts; }  double bcf_stream_qual(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->rec || bcf_float_is_missing(st->rec->qual)) return NAN;   return (double) st->rec->qual; }  int bcf_stream_nsamples(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->hdr) return 0;   return bcf_hdr_nsamples(st->hdr); }  const char *bcf_stream_sample(void *ptr, int idx) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;   if (!st || !st->hdr || idx < 0 || idx >= bcf_hdr_nsamples(st->hdr)) return NULL;   return st->hdr->samples[idx]; }  void bcf_stream_close(void *ptr) {   bcf_stream_t *st = (bcf_stream_t *) ptr;    if (!st) return;    if (st->rec) bcf_destroy(st->rec);   if (st->hdr) bcf_hdr_destroy(st->hdr);   if (st->fp) hts_close(st->fp);   free(st->alts);   memset(st, 0, sizeof(bcf_stream_t));   free(st); } '    ffi <- tcc_ffi()   ffi <- tcc_include(ffi, system.file("include", package = "Rtinycc"))    for (path in pkg_config_paths("--cflags-only-I", "-I")) {     ffi <- tcc_include(ffi, path)   }   for (path in pkg_config_paths("--libs-only-L", "-L")) {     ffi <- tcc_library_path(ffi, path)   }    ffi |>     tcc_source(code) |>     tcc_library("hts") |>     tcc_bind(       bcf_stream_open = list(args = list("cstring"), returns = "ptr"),       bcf_stream_resume = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "i32"),       bcf_stream_done = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "bool"),       bcf_stream_error = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "cstring"),       bcf_stream_chrom = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "cstring"),       bcf_stream_pos1 = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "i64"),       bcf_stream_end1 = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "i64"),       bcf_stream_id = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "cstring"),       bcf_stream_n_allele = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "i32"), returns = "cstring"),       bcf_stream_qual = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "f64"),       bcf_stream_nsamples = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "i32"),       bcf_stream_sample = list(args = list("ptr", "i32"), returns = "cstring"),       bcf_stream_close = list(args = list("ptr"), returns = "void")     ) |>     tcc_compile() }  new_bcf_reader <- function(path, ffi = build_streaming_bcf_ffi()) {   path <- normalizePath(path, mustWork = TRUE)   ptr <- ffi$bcf_stream_open(path)   if (tcc_ptr_is_null(ptr)) {     stop("failed to allocate BCF stream", call. = FALSE)   }    err <- ffi$bcf_stream_error(ptr)   if (!is.null(err)) {     ffi$bcf_stream_close(ptr)     stop(err, call. = FALSE)   }    reader <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())   reader$ffi <- ffi   reader$ptr <- ptr   reader$closed <- FALSE   class(reader) <- "bcf_stream_reader"    reg.finalizer(reader, function(x) {     if (!isTRUE(x$closed)) {       x$ffi$bcf_stream_close(x$ptr)       x$closed <- TRUE     }   }, onexit = TRUE)    reader }  close.bcf_stream_reader <- function(con, ...) {   if (!inherits(con, "bcf_stream_reader")) {     stop("expected a bcf_stream_reader", call. = FALSE)   }   if "bcf_stream_reader"), !isTRUE(reader$closed))   n <- reader$ffi$bcf_stream_nsamples(reader$ptr)   if (n <= 0L) return(character())   vapply(seq_len(n) - 1L, function(i) reader$ffi$bcf_stream_sample(reader$ptr, i), "") }  bcf_reader_next <- function(reader) {   stopifnot(inherits(reader, "bcf_stream_reader"), !isTRUE(reader$closed))    status <- reader$ffi$bcf_stream_resume(reader$ptr)   if (identical(status, 0L)) {     return(NULL)   }   if (identical(status, -1L)) {     err <- reader$ffi$bcf_stream_error(reader$ptr) %||% "BCF stream failed" reader$ffi$bcf_stream_alt(reader$ptr),     qual = reader$ffi$bcf_stream_qual(reader$ptr),     alleles = alleles   ) }  bcf_reader_collect <- function(reader, n = Inf) {   out <- list()   i <- 0L   repeat {     if (i >= n) break     rec <- bcf_reader_next(reader)     if (is.null(rec)) break     i <- i + 1L     out[[i]] <- rec   }   out }  write_demo_vcf <- function(path) {   lines <- c(     "##fileformat=VCFv4.3",     "##contig=<ID=chr1,length=1000>",     "##FORMAT=<ID=GT,Number=1,Type=String,Description=\"Genotype\">", "chr1\t30\trs3\tTT\tT\t.\tPASS\t.\tGT\t0/1"   )   writeLines(lines, path)   invisible(path) }  make_demo_vcf <- function() {   vcf <- tempfile(fileext = ".vcf")   write_demo_vcf(vcf)   vcf } } bcf_stream_t;  static void bcf_stream_set_error(bcf_stream_t *st, const char *msg) {   if (!st) return;   if (!msg) msg = "unknown htslib error";   snprintf(st->err, sizeof(st->err), "%s", msg);   st->error = 1; }  static int bcf_stream_resume_internal(bcf_stream_t *st) {   PT_BEGIN(&st->pt);    if (!st || !st->fp || !st->hdr || !st->rec) {     if (st) bcf_stream_set_error(st, "stream is not open");     if (st) st->done = 1; title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: header-driven bindings, use treesitter.c parse function signatures generate binding specifications automatically. struct, enum, global helpers, tcc_generate_bindings() handles code generation. default mapper conservative pointers: char* treated ptr C guarantee NUL-terminated strings. know parameter C string, provide custom mapper returns cstring type.
code: header <- ' double sqrt(double x); double sin(double x); struct point { double x; double y; }; enum status { OK = 0, ERROR = 1 }; int global_counter; '  tcc_treesitter_functions(header) #>   capture_name text start_line start_col params return_type #> 1    decl_name sqrt          2         8 double      double #> 2    decl_name  sin          3         8 double      double tcc_treesitter_structs(header) #>   capture_name  text start_line #> 1  struct_name point          4 tcc_treesitter_enums(header) #>   capture_name   text start_line #> 1    enum_name status          5 tcc_treesitter_globals(header) #>   capture_name           text start_line #> 1  global_name global_counter          6  # Bind parsed functions to libm symbols <- tcc_treesitter_bindings(header) math <- tcc_link("m", symbols = symbols) math$sqrt(16.0) #> [1] 4  # Generate struct/enum/global helpers ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source(header) |>   tcc_generate_bindings(     header,     functions = FALSE, structs = TRUE,     enums = TRUE, globals = TRUE   ) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi$struct_point_new() #> <pointer: 0x5b3080f28470> ffi$enum_status_OK() #> [1] 0 ffi$global_global_counter_get() #> [1] 0

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: Named nested struct fields can now declared explicitly struct:<name>. Getters return borrowed nested views setters copy bytes another struct object declared nested type. treesitter-generated bindings, nested struct fields inside structs still fall back ptr-like accessors. want borrowed nested view plus copy-setter, declare nested field explicitly struct:<name>. Bitfields separate ordinary addressable fields. use scalar helper accessors, field_addr() container_of() reject bitfield members.
code: ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source('     struct inner { int a; };     struct outer { struct inner in; };   ') |>   tcc_struct("inner", accessors = c(a = "i32")) |>   tcc_struct("outer", accessors = list(`in` = "struct:inner")) |>   tcc_compile()  outer <- ffi$struct_outer_new() inner <- ffi$struct_inner_new() inner <- ffi$struct_inner_set_a(inner, 42L) outer <- ffi$struct_outer_set_in(outer, inner) inner_view <- ffi$struct_outer_get_in(outer) ffi$struct_inner_get_a(inner_view) #> [1] 42 ffi$struct_inner_free(inner) #> NULL ffi$struct_outer_free(outer) #> NULL ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source('struct flags { unsigned int flag : 1; };') |>   tcc_struct(     "flags",     accessors = list(flag = list(type = "u8", bitfield = TRUE, width = 1))   )  tcc_field_addr(ffi, "flags", "flag") #> Error: #> ! field_addr does not support bitfield members tcc_container_of(ffi, "flags", "flag") #> Error: #> ! container_of does not support bitfield members

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: Array fields require list(type = ..., size = N, array = TRUE) syntax tcc_struct(), generates element-wise accessors.
code: ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source('struct buf { unsigned char data[16]; };') |>   tcc_struct("buf", accessors = list(     data = list(type = "u8", size = 16, array = TRUE)   )) |>   tcc_compile()  b <- ffi$struct_buf_new() ffi$struct_buf_set_data_elt(b, 0L, 0xCAL) #> <pointer: 0x5b307db81e00> ffi$struct_buf_set_data_elt(b, 1L, 0xFEL) #> <pointer: 0x5b307db81e00> ffi$struct_buf_get_data_elt(b, 0L) #> [1] 202 ffi$struct_buf_get_data_elt(b, 1L) #> [1] 254 ffi$struct_buf_free(b) #> NULL

title: Builds the TinyCC Command-Line Interface and Library for C Scripting in R
text: Compiled FFI objects fork-safe: parallel::mclapply() fork()-based parallelism work box TCC’s compiled code lives memory mappings survive fork() via copy--write. Serialization also supported. tcc_compiled object stores FFI recipe internally, saveRDS() / readRDS() (serialize() / unserialize()), first $ access detects dead TCC state pointer recompiles transparently. explicit control, use tcc_recompile(). Note raw tcc_state objects bare pointers tcc_malloc() carry recipe remain dead deserialization.
code: ffi <- tcc_ffi() |>   tcc_source("int square(int x) { return x * x; }") |>   tcc_bind(square = list(args = list("i32"), returns = "i32")) |>   tcc_compile()  ffi$square(7L) #> [1] 49  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") saveRDS(ffi, tmp) ffi2 <- readRDS(tmp) unlink(tmp)  # Auto-recompiles on first access ffi2$square(7L) #> [Rtinycc] Recompiling FFI bindings after deserialization #> [1] 49

title: Declare a global variable getter — tcc_global
text: Globals limited scalar FFI types. Array types rejected. Scalar conversions follow rules wrapper arguments: Integer inputs (i8, i16, i32, u8, u16) must finite within range; NA values error. Large integer types (i64, u32, u64) mediated R numeric (double). Values must integer-valued within range; i64/u64 exact integers $2^53$ accepted. Getter wrappers i64/u64 warn stored value exceeds R's exact integer range numeric vectors. bool rejects NA logicals. Ownership notes: ptr globals store raw address external pointer. external pointer owns memory, keep alive; otherwise pointer may freed global still points . cstring globals store borrowed pointer R's string data (UTF-8 translation). free ; C-owned strings prefer ptr global manage lifetime explicitly (e.g., tcc_cstring()).

title: Rtinycc 0.1.11.9000
text: Add tcc_list_symbols() inspect global symbol names resolved hexadecimal addresses known libtcc state. Improve ALTREP-aware copy-paths using RAW_GET_REGION() copying raw vectors native memory scalar accessors callback/struct scalar conversions. Clarify mutable array FFI inputs can materialize ALTREP vectors R exposes writable C storage. Harden async callback draining explicit drains execute R callbacks recorded main R thread, direct main-thread async scheduling executes immediately instead queueing waiting , async trampoline scheduling failures recorded without calling R APIs worker-capable code.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.11
text: Fix PROTECT memory-balance hygiene bugs internal C state initialization (RC_libtcc_state_new, RC_libtcc_get_symbol, callback registration paths). Previously, class string attributes inadvertently allocated without proper protection. Fix stack-depth protection imbalance bug RC_invoke_callback_internal callback execution unexpectedly unprotect call object prior evaluation.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.10
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-27 Fix installation musl-based Linux systems, including Alpine Linux, enabling TinyCC’s musl configuration musl libc environment detected configure. Fix configure failure macOS oldrel arm64 (Big Sur, clang 14). bundled TinyCC Makefile pairs -flat_namespace -undefined warning clang older 15. package strips -flat_namespace macOS avoid TinyCC SIGSEGV behavior flat namespace, clang < 15 left -undefined warning , modern macOS linker rejects default -twolevel_namespace. configure step now also strips -undefined warning libtcc.dylib link succeeds CRAN macOS oldrel arm64 builder.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.9
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-27 Replace upstream make install step explicit copies build artifacts (tcc, libtcc.{,dylib,}, libtcc1., libtcc.h, bundled tinycc C headers lib/tcc/include/). sidesteps BSD/GNU install differences strict install-unx macros upstream Makefile cause configuration fail macOS arm64 x86_64 CRAN builders. installed layout inst/tinycc/ unchanged. Linux, prefer unversioned libm.GNU ld script bare versioned SONAME (libm..6) resolving system math runtime vignettes tests. TinyCC handles GNU ld scripts directly, avoids link failures Fedora-style toolchains. Make external-library linking example tests “Linking External Libraries” vignette skip cleanly tcc_link() resolve platform math runtime, instead erroring whole run. Skip runtime benchmark vignette chunks depend memory profiling running R built without Rprofmem(), avoiding vignette rebuild failures platforms built without memory profiling support. Add CRAN version badge README.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.8
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-24 Clean vignette sources removing package-attachment checks replacing hidden library(Rtinycc) setup explicit namespace bindings, keeping executable examples working vignette builds.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.7
text: Add missing return-value documentation across exported helpers print methods generated reference pages consistently describe result class, meaning, side-effect-behavior. Update vignettes ensure article executes ordinary R code builds, correct conservative type-mapping docs const char * examples reflect default ptr mapping.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.6
text: Exclude top-level scripts/ directory explicitly package builds avoid CRAN notes non-standard top-level contents.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.5
text: Fix Debian CRAN incoming failures around external-library linking. Short linker names now stay normal linker-name path, Unix math-library vignette/test examples now resolve real runtime library file robustly Linux macOS. Improve external library discovery searching relevant loader linker environment paths addition built-platform library path tables. Improve vignette rendering generated C code reusing package’s custom knitr C rendering path highlighted generated code matches vignette C blocks. Clean README rendering around nested-struct bitfield limitations. Add Docker helper reproducing Debian R-devel CRAN-style check locally.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.4
text: Tighten FFI soundness around ownership, helper generation, nested composite views. Generated wrappers now route pointer composite wrapper creation host-side helpers consistently, borrowed/unowned pointer semantics enforced strictly, callback/SEXP/pointer wrapper invariants covered stronger codegen runtime tests. Clarify extend helper semantics across structs, unions, enums, globals, bitfields, treesitter-derived bindings. Helper metadata now records helper family helper operation explicitly, bitfield constant helpers distinguished ordinary field helpers, named nested struct helper accessors support borrowed nested views plus copy-setters. Improve cross-platform robustness generated external-pointer/finalizer paths Windows diagnostics, including host-side registration helpers, tighter smoke tests, CI/debug workflow coverage historical Windows GC/finalizer crash path. Refresh README, vignettes, generated reference documentation user docs match current FFI semantics pointers, callbacks, bitfields, nested helpers, treesitter-derived accessor behavior.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.3
text: Tighten FFI boundary handling wide integers pointers. Generated wrappers, struct accessors, typed memory writers, callback return paths now warn error consistently i64 / u64 values represented exactly R numeric vectors, typed pointer offsets now validated non-negative whole numbers instead truncating int. Extend async callback support i64, u32, u64 arguments use numeric marshaling path async returns, matching documentation 2^53 exactness limit R doubles. Update array callback code generation avoid repeated evaluation additional cstring boxing path, use read-character_array access via STRING_PTR_RO() available, improve callback pointer classification non-string pointer types. Clarify user-facing documentation callback limits draining behavior, character_array semantics, serialization external pointers, benchmark vignette’s zero-length array-return example. Fix FFI return code generation array, cstring, i64, u64 return expressions evaluated boxing/copying. avoids repeated calls generated wrappers side-effectful expressions. Declare temporary cstring return variable const char* generated wrapper accepts const char*-returning C functions without const-discarding assignment. restores vignette builds macOS Windows (bundled TinyCC treated implicit const-discard fatal diagnostic rather warning). Add regression tests repeated-evaluation bugs generated FFI return code. Add make vig make vig-md development targets rebuilding HTML package vignettes Markdown vignette exports. Add internal knitr engine reusable C chunks vignettes, adapted callme package, use benchmark vignette. executable callme comparisons now degrade gracefully build environment compile temporary helper DLL, restores passing package checks Windows CI.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.1
text: Fix C compiler warnings package sources using strict prototypes zero-argument routines explicit initialization async callback result structs. Remove compiler-specific diagnostic pragmas host-symbol registration path package checks longer report pragma usage notes. Rework benchmark documentation temporary callme DLLs created, benchmarked, unloaded within helper scopes. reduces risk Windows vignette-build failures caused lingering loaded modules.

title: Rtinycc 0.1.0
text: Add variadic FFI support tcc_bind() legacy typed-tail mode (variadic = TRUE, varargs) bounded dynamic-tail mode (variadic = TRUE, varargs_types, varargs_min, varargs_max). Refactor FFI codegen callbacks use lambda.r guard rules instead long /else switch chains. keeps type-mapping logic centralized R/aaa_ffi_codegen_rules.R, makes behavior easier extend, reduces duplication across codegen sites. Reduce shutdown-related crash risk using onexit = FALSE registered external-pointer finalizers, cleanup runs normal garbage collection rather R process teardown. change part Windows stabilization work complements explicit ownership tracking TCCState* wrappers. Treesitter helper examples now wrapped \dontrun{} treesitter helper tests skipped Windows avoid process-exit crashes related treesitter.c cleanup. Experimental Windows support. package now builds passes R CMD check Windows (Rtools 4.5 / UCRT). Windows build compiles TinyCC source, dynamically links libtcc.dll, includes compatibility glue needed R’s UCRT runtime. Async callbacks fork()-based parallelism remain Unix-. RC_invoke_callback_id() replaces snprintf-based callback dispatch. trampoline now passes callback token integer directly, eliminating round-trip string conversion relied snprintf (unavailable direct symbol Windows/UCRT). Add new user documentation core FFI semantics, internals, external library linking, treesitter-based header parsing, benchmark-oriented comparisons conventional .Call() workflow.

title: Rtinycc 0.0.3
text: Add typed memory read/write helpers inspired Bun’s FFI ctypesio package: tcc_read_i8(), tcc_read_u8(), tcc_read_i16(), tcc_read_u16(), tcc_read_i32(), tcc_read_u32(), tcc_read_i64(), tcc_read_u64(), tcc_read_f32(), tcc_read_f64(), tcc_read_ptr() corresponding tcc_write_*() functions. operate byte offset use memcpy internally alignment safety. Legacy vectorised interface preserved: tcc_read_u8(ptr, n = 4), tcc_read_i32(ptr, n = 2), tcc_read_f64(ptr, n = 2) continue work alongside new scalar offset API. Fix macOS “undefined symbol” errors relocation time. RC_libtcc_add_host_symbols() explicitly registers host symbols (RC_free_finalizer, RC_invoke_callback, RC_callback_async_schedule_c) via tcc_add_symbol() tcc_relocate(). Fix macOS library linking: short library names like "m" resolved file path now passed -l<name> linker instead erroring. Fix stdbool.h found macOS: tcc_set_lib_path() now receives correct lib/tcc directory TCC resolves headers via {B}/include. tcc_compiled objects now survive serialize()/unserialize() saveRDS()/readRDS(). FFI recipe stored inside compiled object; first access deserialization, $.tcc_compiled detects dead TCC state pointer recompiles transparently. Compiled FFI objects fork-safe: parallel::mclapply() fork()-based parallelism work box since TCC’s compiled code lives memory mappings survive fork() via copy--write.

title: Rtinycc 0.0.2
text: Update package title description Mark first release API versioning going forward

title: Rtinycc 0.0.1.9000 (development version)
text: Generate callback trampolines callback:<signature> bindings C APIs can accept function pointers directly Add callback_async:<signature> thread-safe scheduling main thread Callback errors now warn return type-appropriate defaults (avoid longjmp) Update README examples callbacks, async callbacks, enums, bitfields Fix potential exit-time segfaults using package-level finalizer (RC_free_finalizer) generated struct/union helpers instead JIT-local finalizers Add treesitter.c helpers (tcc_treesitter_functions(), tcc_treesitter_structs(), tcc_treesitter_bindings()) aligned Rtinycc type mappings Require treesitter.c (>= 0.0.3) header parsing helpers Add global getter/setter helpers via tcc_global() generated global_<name>_get() global_<name>_set() accessors BREAKING: struct/union helper names now use struct_<name>_* union_<name>_* prefixes (including bitfields) Add tcc_generate_bindings() expanded tree-sitter helpers enum/union/global parsing binding generation Add pointer utilities: tcc_null_ptr(), tcc_malloc(), tcc_free(), tcc_read_*() helpers, pointer--pointer utilities (tcc_data_ptr(), tcc_ptr_set(), tcc_ptr_free_set_null()), ownership checks via tcc_ptr_is_owned() Tag external pointers owned/borrowed guard frees based ownership Add array field element helpers structs (struct_<name>_get_<field>_elt() / struct_<name>_set_<field>_elt()) Add array return specifications tcc_bind() via returns = list(type = "*_array", length_arg = ..., free = TRUE)

title: Rtinycc 0.0.1
text: First release

title: Rtinycc 0.0.0.9000 (development version)
text: Added bun:ffi inspired FFI API Add R callback API: tcc_callback(), tcc_callback_ptr(), tcc_callback_close() trampoline support passing R functions compiled C code Add structured type support: declarative tcc_struct() tcc_union() generated allocation/accessor helpers introspection (sizeof/alignof) Bitfield support (compiled TinyCC exposed via accessors) improved type mappings (scalars, zero-copy arrays, pointers) Modify configure script alter tinycc Makefile remove flat_namespace option macOS add simple return type tcc_call_symbol
